inputs.name: fileName
inputs.description: The absolute path name of the file to which to write the byte array.

inputs.name: bytes
inputs.description: The byte array to write to the file specified in the filename parameter.

inputs.name: append
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies whether to append or overwrite if the specified file already exists. The default behavior is to create a new file if file does not exist or overwrite an existing file if file already exists. Set to: true to append to the file if the file already exists. false to overwrite the file if the file already exists.

outputs.name: length
outputs.description: Number of bytes written to the file.

inputs.name: fileName
inputs.description: The absolute path name of the file to be checked.

outputs.name: exists
outputs.description: Indicates whether the specified fileName exists or not. A value of: true indicates that the specified fileName exists. false indicates that the specified fileName does not exist.

outputs.name: isDirectory
outputs.description: Indicates whether the specified fileName is a file or a directory. true indicates that the specified fileName is a directory. false indicates that the specified fileName is a file.

inputs.name: fileName
inputs.description: The absolute path name of the file to be copied.

inputs.name: targetDirectory
inputs.description: Directory to which the file specified in the fileName parameter will be copied.

inputs.name: appendTimestamp
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies whether the current timestamp will be appended to the target filename. Set to: true to append a timestamp to the target fileName in the yyyyMMddHHmmss format. false to omit a timestamp from the target fileName.

outputs.name: targetFileName
outputs.description: The absolute path name of the target file to which the fileName parameter is copied.

inputs.name: fileName
inputs.description: The absolute path name of the file to be deleted.

outputs.name: status
outputs.description: Specifies the status of the delete operation. A value of: true indicates that the deletion succeeded. false indicates that the deletion failed.

inputs.name: filename
inputs.description: The absolute path name of the file in the local file system (for example c:\rubicon\document.xml).

inputs.name: loadAs
inputs.description: Optional. Form in which you want the getFile service to make the contents of the file available to subsequent services. Set to: bytes to return the file as a byte array. Use this option if the contents of the file will be used as input to a service that operates on whole documents (for example, pub.xml:queryXMLNode ). This is the default. stream to return the file as an input stream. Use this option if the contents of the file will be used as input to a service that can process a document incrementally (for example, pub.xml:getXMLNodeIterator ). string to return the file as a string. reader to return the file as a reader.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: Optional. Character set in which the file is encoded. Specify an IANA-registered character set (for example, ISO-8859-1). This information is required to correctly convert the String object to bytes when performing a get. If no value is specified or if the value is set to autoDetect, the service uses the default operating system encoding. If you specify an unsupported encoding, the system throws an exception.

inputs.name: bufferSize
inputs.description: Optional. Buffer size (in bytes) to use if you are loading an InputStream (that is, loadAs = stream ). The default is 4096 bytes.

inputs.name: largeFile
inputs.description: Optional. Indicates whether the file specified in the input is a large file or not. As large files are loaded as a stream , set this parameter to true and the loadAs parameter to stream to get larger files. The default value is false . Note: Files larger than the system memory are considered as large files.

outputs.name: body
outputs.description: Document (IData object) containing the file as a byte[ ], InputStream, string, or reader. The body parameter will contain one of the following keys, depending on how the loadAs parameter was set:

inputs.name: directory
inputs.description: Name of the directory that is to be queried.

inputs.name: filter
inputs.description: Optional. Filter string for the file list (for example, *.java or EDI*.tx*).

outputs.name: fileList
outputs.description: Names of the files in the directory specified in the directory parameter. Only the file names are returned; not the full path name. filel]List does not list subdirectories or recursively list subdirectory content.

outputs.name: numFiles
outputs.description: Number of files in the specified directory.

inputs.name: fileName
inputs.description: The absolute path name of the file to be moved.

inputs.name: targetDirectory
inputs.description: Name of the directory to which the file specified in the fileName parameter is to be moved.

inputs.name: appendTimeStamp
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies whether the current timestamp is to be appended to the file name after the file is moved to the target directory. Set to: false if you do not want to append a timestamp. This is the default. true if you want to append a timestamp in the following format: yyyyMMddHHmmss.

outputs.name: status
outputs.description: Status of the move. A value of: true indicates that the move was successful. false indicates that the move failed.

outputs.name: targetFileName
outputs.description: Fully qualified path of the target file.

inputs.name: fileName
inputs.description: Fully qualified name of the file to which the data is to be written.

inputs.name: reader
inputs.description: The reader object from which the data is read.

inputs.name: append
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies whether to append to or overwrite the file if it already exists. The default behavior is to create a new file if the file does not exist or overwrite an existing file if the file already exists. Set to: true to append if the file already exists. false to overwrite if the file already exists.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: Optional. Name of a registered, IANA character set (for example, ISO-8859-1). If you specify an unsupported encoding, the system throws an exception. If no value is specified or if the encoding is set to autoDetect , the default operating system encoding is used.

inputs.name: fileName
inputs.description: Fully qualified name of the file to be written to.

inputs.name: stream
inputs.description: The stream from which data is read.

inputs.name: append
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies whether to append or overwrite if the specified file already exists. The default behavior is to create a new file if the file does not exist or overwrite an existing file if the file already exists. Set to: true to append if the file already exists. false to overwrite if the file already exists.

inputs.name: fileName
inputs.description: Fully qualified name of the file to be written to.

inputs.name: data
inputs.description: Text to be written.

inputs.name: append
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies whether to append or overwrite if the specified file already exists. The default behavior is to create a new file if the file does not exist or overwrite an existing file if the file already exists. Set to: true to append if the file already exists. false to overwrite if the file already exists.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: Optional. Name of a registered, IANA character set (for example, ISO-8859-1). If you specify an unsupported encoding, the system throws an exception. If no value is specified or if the encoding is set to autoDetect , the default operating system encoding is used.
